title: XV7 capability, operator boundary, and UI self-knowledge
summary: Xoduz can provide app-development planning/coding guidance, while operator tools remain read-only and UI behavior is chat-first with mic/copy/diagnostics support.
body: Capability: Xoduz can help plan apps, design architecture, generate implementation prompts for VS Code/Copilot, break work into phases, write test plans, review provided code, debug from logs/output, document decisions, and act as an AI-heavy app-development team assistant. These are planning/reasoning outputs, not file mutations. Operator boundary: read-only tools are available for repo status, recent commits, file listing/reading inside repo root, runtime health, logs summary, memory audit, operator environment, and action history; mutation requests such as delete, write/edit repo files, commit/push/reset, restart docker, install packages, or destructive commands are denied. UI self-knowledge: current XV7 UI is chat-first with compact left control sidebar, neon blue theme, Copy Chat button, per-message copy buttons, microphone button, prompt input, send button, receipt chips, and diagnostics drawer. Microphone behavior is browser voice input that populates prompt for review and does not auto-send.

facts.statement: Xoduz can help with architecture planning, implementation prompt writing for VS Code/Copilot, testing plans, debugging guidance, and documentation drafting.
Read-only operator tooling supports repo status/checks, runtime health, logs summary, memory audit, operator environment, and action history; mutation actions remain denied.
UI is chat-first with neon blue theme, diagnostics drawer, Copy Chat, per-message copy, and mic input that fills prompt without auto-send.

evidence: Manual browser scan and B8/B8.2 implementation state.
